title: sagina procumbens subsp. littoralis
intro: Sagina procumbens subsp. littoralis, also known as shore pearlwort, is a low-growing, perennial plant species native to the coastal regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary issues, such as kidney stones and gravel, due to its high concentration of oxalate crystals, which act as a natural diuretic. The health benefits of shore pearlwort include its ability to help alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and promote the removal of waste products from the body. Herbal preparations of shore pearlwort can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support kidney health and prevent the formation of kidney stones.
